user_query:
I need standardized, tappable menu payloads for our chat assistant based on the requirements in input/menu_requirements.yaml. Please read that file and produce two prompts:

1) A compact Yes/No confirmation using short button labels and one-line option descriptions. Include compact callback_data JSON with a short action key and an optional id (keys limited to "a" and "id"). Keep callback_data strings under 64 bytes.

2) A single-row, 6-option numbered menu with buttons labeled 1–6 in order, with each option on one line in the message body. Use compact callback_data as above.

For channels without inline buttons, also create fallback text for each prompt that lists options in the same order with numbered lines and ends with the exact line: "Reply with the number to choose.".

Write the following files:
- output/prompt_yesno.json — object with keys: message (string) and buttons (array of one row with button objects {text, callback_data}).
- output/prompt_yesno_fallback.txt — numbered list fallback for the Yes/No prompt.
- output/prompt_menu.json — object with keys: message (string with 6 numbered lines) and buttons (array of one row with six button objects {text, callback_data}).
- output/prompt_menu_fallback.txt — numbered list fallback for the menu.

Constraints:
- Button label length must be 1–3 characters.
- Each option description must be a single line.
- Buttons must be provided as a single row (i.e., buttons is an array with exactly one inner array).
- Every callback_data must be a JSON string that parses to an object with key "a" (string, <=20 chars) and optional key "id" (string, <=16 chars), and the overall callback_data string length must be <=64 bytes.
- The fallback files must have the same count of numbered options as the corresponding buttons and end with the exact sentence above.

Make sure the option order in the messages, buttons, and fallbacks matches what’s specified in input/menu_requirements.yaml.
